condition.narrative: The Implementing Partner shall ensure that all of its obligations set forth under the section entitled “Legal Context and/or Risk Management Standard Clauses” are passed on to each responsible party, subcontractor and sub-recipient and that all the clauses under this section entitled “Risk Management” are included, mutatis mutandis, in all sub-contracts or sub-agreements entered into further to the Project Document

Consistent with the Article III of the SBAA [or the Supplemental Provisions to the Project Document], the responsibility for the safety and security of the Implementing Partner and its personnel and property, and of UNDP’s property in the Implementing Partner’s custody, rests with the Implementing Partner. To this end, the Implementing Partner shall: a) put in place an appropriate security plan and maintain the security plan, taking into account the security situation in the country where the project is being carried; b) assume all risks and liabilities related to the Implementing Partner’s security, and the full implementation of the security plan.

Social and environmental sustainability will be enhanced through application of the UNDP Social and Environmental Standards (http://www.undp.org/ses) and related Accountability Mechanism (http://www.undp.org/secu-srm).

description.narrative: Increase in number of successful cases (seizure-arrest-prosecution- conviction) involving wildlife criminals dealing in ivory, rhino horn, pangolins and tiger, and their parts and derivatives . (GWP TT) i) annual number of seizures ii) annual number of arrests iii) ratio of seizures: arrests iv) annual number of prosecutions v) ratio of arrests: prosecutions

comment.narrative: Official national statistics on seizures, arrests and prosecutions for Baseline in 2016*: i): 6 (8.15kg ivory, 427 head/3052 kg pangolins) ii) 1 iii) 6:1 iv) 0 v) 0
Official national statistics on seizures and arrests and prosecutions

Total cases of seizures, arrests, and prosecutions from the base year to June 2023 is as follows: i) No. of seizure 15 exceeding the target of 8 ii) No. of arrest 9, exceeding the target of 3 iii) The ratio is 15:9= 5:3 compared to the baseline of 6:1 iv) No. of prosecution = 3 v) Ratio is 9:3 = 3:1 compared to the baseline of 0

description.narrative: DNP wildlife forensic science laboratory accredited under IS017025 Quality Management System for components of its wildlife DNA forensic testing to align with International Standards and ensure legally admissible evidence for prosecutions in Thailand.

comment.narrative: DNP wildlife forensic science laboratory is not internationally accredited.
DNP wildlife forensic science laboratory is laboratory accredited under IS017025 Quality Management System for components of its wildlife DNA forensic testing
The project board on 3rd of February 2023 approved the replacement of standard compliance from the accreditation under ISO 17025 standard to the SWFS, as the end target achievement.

title.narrative: Social norms and consumer behaviour in key target audiences move towards increased unacceptability of trafficking and purchasing illegal wildlife products.

description.narrative: Identified cases for further investigations and law enforcement actions of DNP through the WARPA framework by assessing through a systematic online wildlife trade monitoring using a standardized methodology.

comment.narrative:
- 20 Facebook (FB) groups monitored
- 3 cases from 2019 monitoring submitted to DNP for further investigation and law enforcement actions

20 FB groups continue to be monitored
- 5 additional (13 cumulative) selected cases submitted to DNP for further investigation and law enforcement actions.

More than 20 Facebook groups have been monitored to a lot larger extent. In this regard, the number of target indicator have been achieved. Evidence of % on-line monitor cases which enter to the law enforcement and prosecution is attached in the upload section.

title.narrative: Social norms and consumer behaviour in key target audiences move towards increased unacceptability of trafficking and purchasing illegal wildlife products

description.narrative: Change in social norms concerning the acceptability of trafficking, buying, possessing and using illegal wildlife products and derivatives as indicated by systematic assessments using a standardized methodology

comment.narrative:
- Zero (0) existing campaigns targeting demand reduction for wildmeat consumption.
- 26% of consumers surveyed think eating wild meat is not socially acceptable (GlobeScan research commissioned by TRAFFIC and ZSL, 2021).
- One campaign targeting demand reduction for wildmeat consumption implemented and evaluated
37% achieved - the target is achieved since 2022.

description.narrative: This project will seek to promote sustainable tourism development in Thailand as a tool to strengthen biodiversity conservation and reduce threats to biodiversity including plastic pollution and climate change. This strategy remains feasible under a COVID-19 context and will capitalize on the opportunity for the government to mainstream biodiversity conservation into its green tourism recovery and resilience strategies.

condition.narrative: Consistent with the Article III of the SBAA [or the Supplemental Provisions to the Project Document], the responsibility for the safety and security of the Implementing Partner and its personnel and property, and of UNDP’s property in the Implementing Partner’s custody, rests with the Implementing Partner. To this end, the Implementing Partner shall: a) put in place an appropriate security plan and maintain the security plan, taking into account the security situation in the country where the project is being carried; b) assume all risks and liabilities related to the Implementing Partner’s security, and the full implementation of the security plan.

title.narrative: Thailand NDC target through incentivizing use of EV

description.narrative: This project was funded by the Government of Japan to explore barriers and enhance enabling conditions to incentivize customers to drive EVs. The objective of the project is to establish the SDG & EV Climate Credit Programme to support the country’s vision on the NDC target and Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) by incentivizing local electric vehicle drivers/organizations to earn credit for driving clean. In addition, the result of the feasibility study of this project will support the adoption of measures that accelerate decarbonization efforts potentially increase carbon emission reduction targets of the transport sector, and foster cooperative relationships between public and private sectors in addressing climate mitigation.

description.narrative: Availability of Thailand’s Greenhouse Gas Emissions Inventory (TGEIS) system that is consistent, accurate and compliant with the MPGs.

comment.narrative: TGEIS system may not fully comply with the Modalities, Procedures, and Guidelines (MPGs) and may have inconsistencies or gaps in activity data and emission factors.

Updates of emission data for the period of 2000-2022, including the calculation method recorded in TGEIS in compliance with the MPGs. The Working Group for National GHG Inventory calculation and 1BTR-5NC/2BTR reporting will be actively involved in the endorsement and review of the data stored in the TGEIS.

title.narrative: Partnership to Accelerate SDG Localization in Thailand

description.narrative: The JP focuses on climate transition, targeting off-track SDGs in an upper-middle-income context by leveraging government resources and UN policy and technical assistance. It will build on the Cooperation Framework and UNCTs work at various levels while partnering with line ministries. The JP enhances awareness and capacities to advance SDG localization at provincial and city levels, involving all 77 Provincial Governors. It will focus on two cities, addressing local climate challenges with technical solutions and developing SDG bankable project proposals. Additionally, it will improve SDG reporting mechanisms by creating tools for provincial and local monitoring, reporting, and planning through Voluntary Local Reviews (VLRs).

Despite the success of SDG-L 1.0 implementation and on-going effective interventions under SDG-L 1.5, several gaps (e.g. income disparity, climate challenges, gender inequality) remain. The proposed project Strengthening Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) Localization in Thailand – Phase II (SDG-L 2.0) aims to advance the effective implementation of SDGs in Thailand, with particular emphasis on human rights, gender equality, environmental sustainability, and climate change adaptation and resilience, including at the subnational level. This initiative will further consolidate the EU-UN-Thailand partnership in the pursuit of Agenda 2030, creating platforms for dialogue among the EU, public authorities, civil society organizations (CSOs), academia, private sector, and other stakeholders across various levels.

title.narrative: Project Preparation grant to finalize the UNDP-GEF project document for project (ABS project)

description.narrative: The objective of the GEF PPG is to develop the project concept into a full project: Building Access and Benefit Sharing (ABS) Framework for Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Use in Thailand. As described in the project concept (PPG Request), this project aims to enhance Thailand’s national framework for ABS and expand community-based ABS models. It addresses gaps in policy, coordination, capacity, and awareness while promoting best practices.

title.narrative: Project preparation grant to finalize the UNDP-GEF project document for project (OECMs project)

description.narrative: The objective of the GEF PPG is to develop the project concept into a full project: Integrated Management of key biodiversity areas and priority species through other effective area-based conservation measures (OECMs) In Thailand. As described in the project concept (PPG Request), this project aims to strengthen biodiversity conservation in Thailand by establishing and managing OECMs across key biodiversity areas, such as the Lower Songkhram River watersheds, Andaman Sea coast, and the Inner Gulf of Thailand.
